title: 10 best herbal creams for leg cramps

remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in Witch Hazel have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation associated with leg cramps.
Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in Witch Hazel, particularly berberine, has been shown to have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle tissue, which can help alleviate cramping in the legs.
Astringent properties: The astringent properties of Witch Hazel can help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which may help relieve symptoms of leg cramps such as numbness and tingling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American Witch Hazel, creams helps with leg cramps because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The natural astringent and antioxidant compounds present in Hamamelis virginiana help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate pain. The cream's cooling sensation also helps to relax the muscles, reducing tension and cramping. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience quick relief from leg cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for this common and often debilitating issue.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help reduce inflammation and spasms in the muscles, providing relief from leg cramps by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation.

Phenolic acids (especially Gallic acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate muscle cramps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.

Hamamelitannin: A unique compound found in Hamamelis virginiana, it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties, which can help reduce blood flow to the affected muscles and alleviate leg cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of hamamelitannin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relieve leg cramps.
Leaves: They are used because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects to alleviate muscle spasms.
Barks: They are used due to their high content of hamamelitannin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers from a trusted source and store them in a cool place.
Steam distill the dried plant material in a 30:1 ratio of water to plant for 2 hours to obtain a fragrant essential oil.
Combine 100g of shea butter with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until the mixture reaches 160°f.
Mix 5ml of the essential oil with 50g of distilled water and add it to the shea butter mixture.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help numb the affected area, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort caused by leg cramps.
Methyl salicylate: Methyl salicylate, a natural derivative of aspirin, helps to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, easing muscle spasms and cramping associated with leg cramps.
Camphor: Camphor's counterirritant properties help to distract the brain from pain signals, providing temporary relief from discomfort and numbness caused by leg cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with leg cramps because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural anti-inflammatory compound. This active ingredient mimics the pain-relieving effects of aspirin, reducing muscle spasms and inflammation that can cause leg cramps. The cream also relaxes tense muscles, allowing for improved blood flow and reduced discomfort. As a result, Gaultheria procumbens creams can provide quick and effective relief from leg cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Volatile oil (especially Gaultherin): This terpene constituent helps with leg cramps by relaxing muscle spasms and reducing inflammation due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Phenolic acids (especially Salicin): These compounds help with leg cramps by reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant properties that mitigate muscle damage.

Gaultherin and its derivatives: These compounds help with leg cramps by acting as a natural muscle relaxant, reducing muscle spasms and pain, and also by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their rich content of salicin, which helps to relieve pain and reduce inflammation associated with leg cramps.
Leaves: They contain compounds with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a suitable addition to creams for treating leg cramp discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome's salicin content and other bioactive compounds help to soothe and relax muscles, alleviating the symptoms of leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems from a clean and organic source.
Dry the harvested leaves and stems in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried gaultheria procumbens in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to create a cream.

remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound found in white willow bark, helps to reduce pain and inflammation by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and swelling.
Glycosides: The glycosides present in white willow cream help to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can contribute to leg cramps.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: White willow creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension, both of which can exacerbate leg cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with leg cramps because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The cream contains salicin, a natural compound that is converted into salicylic acid in the body, similar to aspirin. This action helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles, particularly in the legs, relieving cramping and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's soothing and cooling effects can also help to calm the muscles, providing relief from spasms and cramps.
remedy_constituents: Salicylic acid: It helps with leg cramps by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic, reducing pain and inflammation in the muscles.
Salicin: It helps with leg cramps by acting as a natural muscle relaxant, reducing muscle spasms and cramping by inhibiting the release of certain neurotransmitters.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): They help with leg cramps by acting as natural antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, reducing muscle inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to muscle cramping.
remedy_parts: Barks: Salix alba barks are used due to their high content of salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: Salix alba stems are used because they contain compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce spasms associated with leg cramps.
Leaves: Salix alba leaves are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm muscle cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried salix alba bark and 10g of beeswax at the same time.
Combine 50ml of distilled water and 20g of the salix alba bark in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 30 minutes then remove it from heat and strain.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of shea butter in a separate saucepan.
Combine the two mixtures and blend them until they reach a smooth creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and ease muscle tension caused by leg cramps.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in aloe vera, including vitamins C and E, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to muscle fatigue and cramping.
Wound-healing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's gel has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial agents that promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin, reducing discomfort caused by leg cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with leg cramps because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax and soothe the muscles. The cream's cooling and calming effect can reduce muscle spasms and ease the pain associated with leg cramps. Additionally, aloe vera contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other essential nutrients, which help to nourish and hydrate the skin, reducing the frequency and severity of leg cramps. Its natural ingredients make it an effective and gentle remedy for relief.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, which help to alleviate muscle spasms and reduce pain associated with leg cramps.
Saponins: Aloe vera saponins have been shown to possess anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to calm muscle contractions and reduce the frequency and severity of leg cramps.

Glycoproteins: Aloe vera glycoproteins, such as acemannan, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on muscles, which can help to reduce muscle tension and alleviate symptoms of leg cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramp pain.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is soothing and calming, which helps to relax muscles and ease leg cramp discomfort.
Juice from Leaves: The juice from Aloe vera leaves contains nutrients and compounds that help to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and alleviate leg cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 aloe vera leaves from mature plants at dawn when they are full of water and nutrients.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Extract the gel from the leaves by cutting them open and scooping out the clear inner gel mass.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture thickens.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Arnica cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, reduce inflammation and swelling caused by muscle spasms, which helps alleviate leg cramp pain.

Pain-relieving properties: The presence of sesquiterpenes in arnica cream has been shown to have analgesic (pain-relieving) effects, helping to numb the affected area and provide quick relief from leg cramps.
Muscle relaxant properties: Arnica's flavonoids and other compounds help to relax muscle spasms and reduce muscle tone, which can help to prevent leg cramps from occurring in the first place.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with leg cramps because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The cream's active compounds, including arnica acid and helenin, work together to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramp pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, Arnica montana cream can help relax tense muscles and provide quick relief from painful cramps. This natural remedy is often used topically to soothe and calm the affected area, providing fast and effective relief.
remedy_constituents: Thiophene derivatives: Thiophene derivatives in Arnica montana have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce muscle spasms and cramps by reducing inflammation in the affected area.

Flavonoids (especially kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids in Arnica montana have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate pain and cramps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the muscles.

Tremetol (a sesquiterpene lactone): Tremetol in Arnica montana has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help relieve pain and muscle spasms associated with leg cramps by reducing inflammation and blocking pain pathways.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help alleviate muscle cramps and soreness.
Leaves: They are used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relief from muscle spasms and cramps.
Roots: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and relax muscles affected by cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 arnica montana flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the arnica montana flowers and leaves in a single layer for 7-10 days at 30-40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried arnica montana flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the arnica montana powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and reaches a temperature of 60-70 degrees celsius after 10-15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with leg cramps, allowing for easier movement and relaxation.

The active compounds in Echinacea angustifolia, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, work to relax muscles, reduce muscle spasms, and decrease inflammation, providing relief from leg cramps. remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside and caftaric acid, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: Utilized for their potential in reducing muscle spasms and inflammation, possibly due to the presence of caffeic acid and other compounds.
Stems: Employed for their supposed ability to relax muscles and reduce cramping, possibly attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 20-30 minutes and then let it cool.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help numb the affected area and reduce muscle spasms associated with leg cramps.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol is an expectorant that helps to break down and eliminate excess mucus and toxins from the body, which can contribute to muscle cramps by reducing inflammation and congestion in the muscles.
Camphor: Camphor has a topical analgesic effect, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with leg cramps, while also relaxing muscle spasms and improving circulation to the affected area.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with leg cramps because of its natural muscle relaxant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint creams can help to ease muscle spasms and reduce pain by cooling the affected area and increasing blood flow. Additionally, the menthol can help to break down lactic acid buildup in the muscles, which is a common cause of cramping. remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used in creams for leg cramps due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides a cooling and relaxing effect.
Roots: Mentha x piperita roots are used in creams for leg cramps as they contain a higher concentration of menthol than leaves, making them more effective for pain relief.
Stems: Mentha x piperita stems are used in creams for leg cramps as they contain a mixture of menthol and other compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh menthaxpiperita leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best flavor.
Infuse the harvested leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong tea.
Strain the tea into a clean bowl and discard the solids then let it cool to room temperature.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the cooled tea with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture then whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, helping to alleviate muscle spasms and cramping associated with conditions like fibromyalgia or peripheral neuropathy.

Pain-relieving properties: The sesquiterpenes present in frankincense have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to reduce pain and discomfort caused by leg cramps, while also promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Antioxidant properties: Frankincense's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to muscle cramping and weakness, making it an effective natural remedy for supporting overall muscular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with leg cramps because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that soothe muscle spasms and relax tense muscles. The boswellic acids in frankincense cream reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area, providing quick relief from cramping. Regular application of boswellia serrata creams may also help improve circulation and reduce muscle strain, further alleviating the discomfort associated with leg cramps, promoting overall muscle health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-Keto-β-Boswellic Acid (AKBA): AKBA has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce muscle cramp severity by reducing inflammation and pain in the affected area.
Boswellic acids (BAs): BAs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate muscle cramps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in muscles.
α-Boswellic acid (α-BA): α-BA has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which may help reduce muscle cramp severity by relaxing muscle spasms and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain boswellic acids, which are believed to help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with leg cramps.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata contain boswellic acids, similar to the roots, which may help in alleviating pain and inflammation caused by leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 2 teaspoons of boswellia serrata extract to the mixture and stir well for 1 minute.
Gradually add 4 tablespoons of distilled water to the mixture while whisking continuously for 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal creams containing anti-inflammatory agents like arnica, ginger, or turmeric may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with leg cramps.

Pain-relieving properties: Topical applications containing herbal pain relievers like capsaicin (found in chili peppers) or menthol may help alleviate muscle spasms and numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from leg cramp pain.

Muscle relaxant properties: Herbal ingredients like valerian root, passionflower, or kava may have a calming effect on muscles, which could help relax tense muscles and reduce the frequency of leg cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with leg cramps because they contain capsaicin, a natural pain-relieving compound. When applied topically, capsaicin blocks the release of pain-causing chemicals in the body, providing quick relief from muscle cramps. The cream also increases blood flow to the affected area, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the muscle. This helps to alleviate cramping and discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for people who experience frequent leg cramps.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with leg cramps by blocking the nerve signals that cause muscle contractions, thereby reducing muscle spasms and pain.
Quercetin: Quercetin helps with leg cramps by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to muscle cramps and spasms.
Rotenone: Rotenone helps with leg cramps by acting as a calcium channel blocker, which can help relax muscle contractions and reduce muscle pain.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used to make creams for leg cramps due to their high content of capsaicin, which helps relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: They are used in creams for leg cramps due to their high oil content, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area.
Leaves: They are used in creams for leg cramps due to their high capsaicin content, which helps to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20 grams of dried capsicum annuum powder and store it in a bowl.
Combine the dried capsicum annuum powder with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of shea butter to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool completely before transferring it to a jar.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum (Eucalyptus globulus) contains compounds like eucalyptol, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, alleviating leg cramp pain.

Analgesic properties: The herbal cream may contain analgesics like methyl salicylate, derived from wintergreen oil, which helps numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from leg cramp discomfort and pain.

Muscle relaxant properties: The Eucalyptus globulus extract in Tasmanian Blue Gum creams may also exhibit muscle-relaxing effects, helping to reduce muscle spasms and twitching that can contribute to leg cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with leg cramps because of its unique properties that ease muscle spasms and relax the body. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as cineole and eucalyptol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, reducing pain and swelling in the affected area. When applied topically, these creams can help to reduce muscle cramp frequency and severity by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, providing quick relief and comfort to individuals experiencing leg cramps.

Barks: Eucalyptus bark contains a compound called salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: Eucalyptus stems also contain eucalyptol, which helps to relax muscles and reduce pain associated with leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the mixture and melt them together for 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool for 10 minutes before whipping it with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into a clean container and store it in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with leg cramps because of its unique ability to relax and calm the muscles. The herbal extract, derived from the flowers of Lavandula angustifolia, has a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and twitching that can lead to cramping. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood flow, further alleviating discomfort and pain associated with leg cramps. This natural remedy provides effective relief for those experiencing frequent or severe leg cramp episodes.
remedy_properties: Relaxation and Calming Properties: The calming effects of English lavender help to relax muscle tissue, reducing muscle spasms and cramping.
Antispasmodic Effects: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help to ease muscle contractions and reduce the frequency and severity of leg cramps.

Anti-inflammatory and Pain-Relieving Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in English lavender cream can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with leg cramps, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and relax muscle spasms, making them effective in relieving leg cramps.
Buds: Lavender buds contain a high concentration of essential oils, which provide pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects when used in creams for leg cramp relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground lavender powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat at 60-70°c for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the oil mixture and stir until fully melted, then remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding any solids and leaving behind the lavender-infused oil blend.
Allow the infused oil blend to cool and thicken at room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes until it reaches a smooth cream consistency.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in lemon balm can help to relieve muscle contractions and spasms associated with leg cramps, reducing their frequency and severity.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of calcium, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramping.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers.
Prepare the herbal material by chopping and drying the mixture.
Infuse the herbs in carrier oil using cold pressing.
Strain and filter the infused oil to remove solids.
Mix the herbal oil with beeswax and emulsifiers to create the final cream.

Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens): Devil's Claw contains harpagoside, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, making it potentially effective for relieving leg cramp symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 20% ginkgo biloba extract 2oz of coconut oil 4oz of shea butter.
Melt 2oz of coconut oil and 4oz of shea butter in a double boiler until smooth and creamy.
Add 10ml of 20% ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture and stir well.
Heat the mixture for an additional 15 minutes stirring occasionally to ensure infusion.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers filling to the brim and allow to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with leg cramps because of its unique combination of ingredients that work together to relax muscle spasms. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the dandelion plant, help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots taraxacum officinale weighing approximately 100 grams for processing.
Dry the harvested roots in a dehydrator at 35°c for 12 hours or air dry for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using an electric grinder and store it in an airtight container.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax to create a smooth blend.
Heat the mixture gently at 60°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with leg cramps because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that ease muscle tension and relax spasms. The cream's soothing properties calm the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort associated with leg cramps. Additionally, lemongrass' natural antispasmodic properties help to reduce muscle contractions, allowing for a quicker recovery from cramp episodes. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to alleviate the distressing symptoms of leg cramps, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemongrass cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscle spasms and reduce muscle contractions, which can alleviate symptoms of leg cramps.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemongrass cream can reduce inflammation and swelling associated with leg cramps, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to muscle damage and fatigue, which may exacerbate the occurrence of leg cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in essential oils, rhyzomes are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort associated with leg cramps.

remedy_recipe: Gather and dry high-quality cymbopopgon citratus leaves and stems in a well-ventilated area for two weeks.
Combine one part dried cymbopogon citratus with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the herbal extract into the carrier oil, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the clear oil.
Mix one tablespoon of the cymbopogon citratus infused oil with two tablespoons of beeswax and three tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with leg cramps because of their unique combination of natural ingredients that work together to relax and soothe muscle spasms. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger help reduce swelling and pain, while its warming effect increases blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate cramps. As a result, herbal ginger creams provide fast and effective relief from leg cramps, promoting comfort and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, providing relief from leg cramps.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anti-pyretic properties of ginger help block the transmission of pain signals to the brain, reducing the discomfort associated with leg cramps.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginger's ability to dilate blood vessels can increase blood flow to affected areas, which helps to relax muscle fibers and reduce spasms that cause leg cramps.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the ginger plant, which contains a higher concentration of medicinal properties than other parts of the plant, making it effective in relieving leg cramp pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Grind 100g of dried ginger into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground ginger powder with 50g of beeswax and 25g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Add 10ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until well combined for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with leg cramps because of its natural ability to regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly in women. Muscle relaxant properties: The herbal extract in chaste tree creams has been shown to have a sedative effect on muscles, helping to relax tense muscles and reduce muscle spasms that cause leg cramps.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried vitex agnus castus flowers measuring one part by weight of dried flowers to four parts by weight of sweet almond oil.
Combine the dried vitex agnus castus flowers with 100 milliliters of glycerin and 50 grams of distilled water in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days in a warm dark place, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Blend the liquid extract with 200 grams of beeswax pellets, heating the mixture in a double boiler until melted and fully incorporated at 160 degrees fahrenheit for one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams helps with leg cramps because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to relax muscle spasms. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in roselle cream can help reduce swelling and pain in muscles, reducing the severity of leg cramps.
Muscle-relaxant properties: Roselle contains flavonoids that have been shown to relax smooth and skeletal muscles, helping to alleviate muscle spasms and cramps.

Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe muscle spasms and ease pain caused by leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hibiscus sabdariffa flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the hibiscus flowers using a food dehydrator or sunlight for 24 hours.
Blend 100g of dried hibiscus flowers with 200ml of coconut oil in a coffee grinder.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 30 minutes before straining.
Whip 500g of raw shea butter with 20% of the infused oil mixture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg creams helps with leg cramps because it contains natural ingredients that promote relaxation and ease muscle spasms. Muscle relaxant property: Nutmeg has been found to have a calming effect on muscles, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve pain associated with leg cramps.

Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, relieving pain and cramping associated with leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried myristica fragrans seeds from a trusted source for optimal potency.
Combine 20 grams of the dried myristica fragrans seeds with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to allow for thorough infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool completely before whipping it with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil for 10 minutes until smooth and creamy.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for leg cramps are topical preparations containing natural ingredients that help alleviate muscle spasms and cramps in the legs. These creams work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation, providing relief from excruciating pain. The best herbal creams for leg cramps include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Piper nigrum, Lavandula angustifolia, Ammi majus, Vitex agnus-castus, Gelsemium sempervirens, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, leg cramps can significantly disrupt daily life, causing insomnia, fatigue, and decreased mobility, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to leg cramps that are treated with herbal creams are muscle spasms, joint pain, and menstrual cramps. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, peppermint, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to relieve discomfort and reduce pain. These creams can be applied topically to affected areas to provide quick relief from symptoms without the need for oral medication.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with leg cramps is a blend of arnica and peppermint. Arnica, a natural anti-inflammatory, soothes and calms the affected area, reducing pain and swelling. Peppermint, with its cooling properties, helps relax the muscles and ease cramping. Adding a small amount of ginger cream to this combination can further enhance its effectiveness, as ginger's warming properties can help increase blood flow and reduce spasms. This trio of creams can provide quick and long-lasting relief from leg cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with leg cramps because of its relaxing and soothing properties. The root of the Valeriana plant contains valerenic acid, which has a calming effect on the muscles. When applied topically, the cream can help to reduce muscle spasms and ease cramping sensations. The anti-inflammatory properties of the cream can also help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with leg cramps. As a result, Valeriana officinalis creams can provide quick relief from leg cramps, making it an effective natural remedy for this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Helps to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, leading to relief from leg cramps.
Valerenic acid: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle contractions that cause leg cramps.
Valeranone: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension that leads to leg cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which have been found to have a sedative effect and help alleviate muscle cramps.
Leaves: The leaves are often used in combination with the roots to enhance the sedative properties and provide relief from muscle spasms.
Stems: The stems are sometimes used, although less frequently than the roots and leaves, to provide additional sedative properties and support muscle relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root and 10 grams of dried flowers at dawn to capture optimal potency.
Combine the dried valeriana officinalis root and flowers in a mortar and pestle to create a uniform mixture.
Steep 200 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler with the valeriana officinalis mixture for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil from the valeriana officinalis mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of the valeriana officinalis infused oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams helps with leg cramps because it contains the extract of the Passiflora incarnata plant, also known as passionflower. The natural compounds in this plant, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax muscles and alleviate cramping. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of Passiflora incarnata can also help to reduce muscle spasms and provide relief from leg cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane acts as a sedative and muscle relaxant, helping to alleviate muscle spasms and cramps in the legs.
Isovitexin: Isovitexin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with leg cramps.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce muscle inflammation and spasms that lead to leg cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used because they are the primary source of the bioactive compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of Passiflora incarnata.
Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of Passifloric acid, a key component in the treatment of leg cramps.
Fruits: The fruit pulp is often utilized because it contains a high amount of vitamins and minerals, which help alleviate muscle cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 100°f for 2-3 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steep 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard solids.
Whip 1 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with leg cramps because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce muscle spasms and relax the muscles, providing relief from cramp pain. The warming sensation of ginger also increases blood flow to the affected area, reducing stiffness and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making Zingiber officinale creams a natural and effective remedy for leg cramps.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help with leg cramps by relaxing muscles, reducing inflammation, and preventing spasms.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-spasmodic properties that help alleviate muscle cramps by inhibiting the contraction of muscle fibers.
Gingerols' active metabolite, Gingerdiol (6-Gingerdiol): It has been found to have an inhibitory effect on the contraction of muscle fibers, thus helping to relieve muscle cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of ginger for medicinal purposes, including creams for leg cramps, due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: The roots of ginger are also widely used, as they contain similar compounds to rhyzomes, providing relief from muscle and joint pain associated with leg cramps.

Stems: The stems of ginger, although less commonly used, may be utilized in creams for leg cramps, offering some anti-inflammatory benefits, although the extent is less pronounced than that of rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Combine 3 tablespoons of distilled water with 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root powder in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the powder is fully dissolved.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for 20 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Whip 8 ounces of heavy cream with 1 teaspoon of the zingiber officinale infusion until it becomes stiff and holds its shape.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with leg cramps because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the muscles. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba help to relax the muscle spasms and reduce the frequency and severity of cramps. Additionally, the cream's anti-oxidant properties help to protect the muscles from oxidative stress, further reducing the occurrence of cramps. This makes Ginkgo biloba creams a popular natural remedy for relieving leg cramps and promoting overall muscle health.

Bilobalide: This constituent helps relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from cramping.
Ginkgolides: They exhibit anti-convulsant properties, which help stabilize muscle contractions and prevent painful cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are used to make creams for leg cramps because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used to make creams for leg cramps because they contain bilobalide and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties and help relax muscles.
Leaves are sometimes replaced by Buds: Some creams may use Ginkgo biloba buds because they contain flavonoids that help improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms, similar to the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and mix 200g of ginkgo biloba extract with 300g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Gradually add 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of jojoba oil to the mixture.
Stir in 20g of vitamin e oil and 5g of lecithin granules into the mixture.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 40°c in about 20 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams helps with leg cramps because they contain the bioactive compound piperine, which has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The piperine in these creams helps to relax the muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing muscle spasms and cramping. Additionally, piperine has been shown to have a natural muscle relaxant effect, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating leg cramps and promoting overall muscle health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: Helps with leg cramps by reducing muscle spasms and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: Contribute to relief from leg cramps by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which reduce muscle inflammation and spasms.
Alkaloids (including Piperlonguminine): Help alleviate leg cramps by acting as muscle relaxants and anti-spasmodic agents, which reduce muscle tension and spasms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds, also known as peppercorns, are commonly used in creams for leg cramps due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Piper nigrum roots are another part used to make creams for leg cramps because they contain compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce pain.
Stems: Piper nigrum stems are also used in creams for leg cramps due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of piper nigrum berries when they are ripe and the color has changed to black.
Steam distill the berries to extract the essential oil for 2 hours using a distillation apparatus.
Combine 150 grams of candelilla wax with 100 grams of cocoa butter and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 10 grams of the extracted essential oil to the melted wax and butter mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with leg cramps because of their anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties. The calming effects of lavender oil, a key ingredient in these creams, can help to soothe and relax muscles, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, the oil's analgesic properties can help to alleviate pain associated with leg cramps, allowing for a more restful night's sleep and improved overall well-being. By reducing muscle tension, these creams can provide relief from recurring leg cramps.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate leg cramp pain.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to muscle cramps and spasms.
Lavandulol: This terpene has a sedative effect and can help to relax muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramps, as well as promoting a good night's sleep to prevent recurring cramps.

Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate pain.
Stems: They contain similar compounds to flowers and leaves, contributing to their anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency and aroma.
Combine the dried flowers with 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 2 hours to allow the herbal extract to infuse into the carrier oil slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Whip 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter with 100ml of the herbal oil mixture to create a rich and moisturizing cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ammi majus creams helps with leg cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The cream is derived from the seeds of the Ammi majus plant, which has been traditionally used to relieve muscle spasms and reduce inflammation. When applied topically, the cream can help relax the muscle fibers, reducing the severity and frequency of leg cramps. remedy_parts: Leaves: Their bioactive compounds, such as coumarins, are used to provide relief from muscle spasms and cramps.

Seeds: Ammi majus seeds contain oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried ammi majus flowers and leaves from a clean and sterile environment.
Combine 20 grams of dried ammi majus with 200 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours at 60 degrees celsius then strain and discard solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of carrier oil in a separate double boiler.
Combine the infused ammi majus oil with the beeswax mixture and whip until thickened to 45 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams helps with leg cramps because they contain bioactive compounds that promote hormonal balance and relaxation of the muscles. The herb's phytoestrogens mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS) and menopause, including leg cramps. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus has a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing muscle spasms and cramps caused by stress, anxiety, or hormonal fluctuations. This natural remedy offers a soothing and calming approach to managing leg cramps.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramps in the legs.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to muscle cramps.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds have a sedative effect and can help regulate the nervous system, which can contribute to the development of muscle cramps.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which help to relax muscles and alleviate cramps.
Seeds: Seeds are used as they contain a significant amount of agnuside, a compound known for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in relieving leg cramps.

Leaves: Leaves are also used as they contain various compounds, including flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which have a relaxing effect on muscles and may help in reducing the frequency and severity of leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers in a clean glass container with 500 ml of 95% ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days while shaking the container twice daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 200 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Mix the extracted liquid with the melted wax and 50 grams of coconut oil then pour into molds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Gelsemium sempervirens creams helps with leg cramps because it is known for its muscle relaxant properties, which ease muscle spasms and cramps. The active compound in Gelsemium sempervirens, gelsemine, has a natural calming effect on the muscles, reducing tension and allowing the body to relax. This natural relief can provide quick and effective pain management, making it an attractive option for those suffering from recurring leg cramps. Regular use may also help prevent future episodes.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: It helps with leg cramps by acting as a calcium channel blocker, which can help regulate muscle contractions and alleviate spasms.
Tannins: Tannins in Gelsemium sempervirens have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce muscle inflammation and pain associated with leg cramps.
Alkaloids (including gelsemine and other compounds): These compounds have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms that lead to leg cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The roots of Gelsemium sempervirens are primarily used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which are believed to have pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Gelsemium sempervirens are used due to their ability to be easily extracted and processed into a form suitable for topical application.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Gelsemium sempervirens are also used due to their ability to be concentrated into a more potent form, which can be further processed into creams for leg cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of fresh gelsemium sempervirens roots and rhizomes in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes in a warm place for at least two weeks or until they become brittle.
Grind 20 grams of dried gelsemium sempervirens roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of the powder in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 20-30 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with leg cramps because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to relax the muscles, reduce spasms, and alleviate pain associated with leg cramps. The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to improve blood flow and reduce muscle tension, providing relief from recurring leg cramps. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help to reduce the frequency and severity of leg cramps, promoting overall muscle health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with leg cramps by reducing inflammation and muscle spasms through its anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties.
Flavonoids: Assists in alleviating leg cramps by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which help to reduce muscle strain and spasms.
Licoricidin: Contributes to the relief of leg cramps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce muscle damage and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to relieve muscle cramps.
Barks: Used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects to calm muscle spasms.
Leaves: Used for their antispasmodic and diuretic properties to relieve fluid retention and ease muscle cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots at the end of the summer season for maximum potency.
Mix 10 grams of the dried roots with 50 grams of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes then let it cool to room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.
Combine the 20% liquid extract with 80% emollient cream base in a stainless steel mixing tank.
